content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:49 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to get this hotfix onto `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push that review branch to the local `origin`.

content.text: I’m going to preserve the current checkout state, then create the review branch, apply the mobile hotfix, and push it to local origin.

Switched to a new branch 'review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix'
review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix

arguments.command: grep -RIn --exclude-dir=.git -E "shape sort|shape-sort|drag target|drag targets|touch target|touch targets|mobile" /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo | head -200

content.text:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>
I found the hotfix point: the game is using 72px tiles and only 6px of grab padding. I’m widening both so finger taps can hit the drag targets more easily.

details.diff:
1 const shapeSortConfig = {
- 2   tileSizePx: 72,
- 3   dragPaddingPx: 6,
- 4   snapTolerancePx: 16,
+ 2   tileSizePx: 88,
+ 3   dragPaddingPx: 18,
+ 4   snapTolerancePx: 20,
  5 };
  6 
  7 window.shapeSortConfig = shapeSortConfig;
  8 
    ...

git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo add public/games/shape-sort/game.js public/games/shape-sort/style.css && git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo commit -m "fix: enlarge shape sort mobile targets" && git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo push -u origin review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix

content.text:
Done — I put the hotfix on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and pushed it to local `origin`.

What changed:
- increased Shape Sort tile size from `72px` to `88px`
- widened drag padding from `6px` to `18px`
- bumped snap tolerance a bit to `20px`
- matched the tile CSS to the larger touch-friendly size

Commit:
- `2098e7d` — `fix: enlarge shape sort mobile targets`

I left the unrelated working tree changes in place (`README.md`, `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md`, `notes/homepage-copy-review.md`, `.env`, `playtest/`).
